sections.heading: Level 1: Camada física e protocolo
sections.bodyMd:
Level 1 valida a interface de comunicação entre o terminal e o cartão: RF para contactless (ISO 14443) ou contato (ISO 7816). Testa timing, modulação, anticolisão e interoperabilidade com cartões de referência. Para SoftPOS/Tap to Mobile, o NFC do dispositivo e o stack de baixo nível precisam passar nos testes do laboratório aprovado.

_EMV® Level 1 (L1) and Level 2 (L2) testing assesses if EMV acceptance devices, mobile payment form factors and EMV Chip cards meet the EMV Contact and Contactless Specifications for performance and compatibility.

_EMV® Level 3 (L3) testing aims to validate the integration of an EMV acceptance device with its acceptance infrastructure to help ensure the interoperability of an end-to-end EMV transaction. _ _In this post, Arman Aygen, EMVCo Director of Technology, covers the basics of EMV L3 testing and how EMVCo supports the delivery of seamless and secure chip card payments around the world.

Before exploring EMV L3 testing, it is important to firstly understand the role of EMV Level 1 (L1) and Level 2 (L2).

**Pontos técnicos:**
- *What products does EMVCo assess? And are there different requirements for EMV L1 and L2 approvals?**
sections.btgpayNote: Certificações L1/L2/L3 e programas de bandeira impactam diretamente o cronograma do POS mobile BtgPay. Alterações no fluxo de pagamento ou no bridge nativo podem exigir reteste em laboratório aprovado.

sections.heading: Level 2: Kernel de aplicação
sections.bodyMd:
Level 2 certifica o kernel EMV: seleção de AID, processamento de GPO, CVM, geração de cryptograma, regras de floor limit e torniquete (torn transaction). O kernel é tipicamente fornecido por um provedor licenciado: o app BtgPay integra via SDK, não reimplementa o kernel.

_EMV® Level 1 (L1) and Level 2 (L2) testing assesses if EMV acceptance devices, mobile payment form factors and EMV Chip cards meet the EMV Contact and Contactless Specifications for performance and compatibility.

EMV L2 refers to the software component, commonly known as the ‘kernel’ or ‘payment application’, that resides on an acceptance device or card and mobile respectively.  This software contains the set of functions that provide the processing logic along with necessary data to perform an EMV contact or contactless transaction.

To ease the transition for stakeholders deploying the new EMV Contactless Kernel, the approval process supports two approaches.  The EMV Contactless Kernel can be tested as a full contactless acceptance device, which reflects the approach currently used for other available contactless kernels.  Additionally, EMVCo has introduced standalone testing of the EMV Contactless Kernel itself to support different deployment options for products in the field that are already approved.

sections.heading: Level 3: Integração adquirente
sections.bodyMd:
Level 3 testa a integração ponta a ponta com a rede do adquirente: mensagens ISO 8583, campos EMV (DE 55), cenários de aprovação/negação, estorno e reconciliação. O time frontend precisa exibir estados corretos e nunca persistir dados sensíveis fora do ambiente certificado.

Before exploring EMV L3 testing, it is important to firstly understand the role of EMV Level 1 (L1) and Level 2 (L2).
